prerequisites: A LinkedIn Ads account with permission to access the advertising data you want to replicate.
A LinkedIn Developer application created in the LinkedIn Developer Portal, with the Advertising API product added and approved (Advertising API access requires explicit LinkedIn approval).
Client ID and Client Secret issued for your LinkedIn application from the app's Auth tab.
A start_date in YYYY-MM-DD format (required); data before this date is not replicated.
Optionally, the specific LinkedIn ad account_ids to sync; if omitted, all accessible accounts are synced.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (client ID, client secret, refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.refresh_token secret fields. LinkedIn uses 3-legged OAuth 2.0: configure your app to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret, run the authorization-code flow to consent, then exchange the code at https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken to obtain tokens. The refresh token is used to renew access without re-authenticating; LinkedIn refresh tokens are available to approved partners and expire after roughly 12 months.

authMethods.name: Access token
authMethods.summary: Maps to the credentials.access_token secret field. Provide a LinkedIn OAuth 2.0 access token directly (for example one generated via the LinkedIn Developer Portal Token Generator). LinkedIn access tokens are issued with a 60-day lifespan and must be renewed manually when they expire.

setupSteps.title: Create a LinkedIn Developer application
setupSteps.body: In the LinkedIn Developer Portal (https://www.linkedin.com/developers/apps), create a new application or select an existing one. Open the Auth tab to view the assigned Client ID and Client Secret, and add an HTTPS redirect (callback) URL that LinkedIn will trust.

setupSteps.title: Request Advertising API access
setupSteps.body: On the application's Products tab, add the Advertising API product. Advertising API access requires explicit approval from LinkedIn before the relevant scopes become available to your app.

setupSteps.title: Obtain OAuth credentials or an access token
setupSteps.body: For OAuth 2.0, run the 3-legged authorization-code flow (GET https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/authorization, then POST the returned code to https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/v2/accessToken with your client_id, client_secret, and redirect_uri) to receive an access_token and refresh_token. Alternatively, generate a single access token manually with the LinkedIn Developer Portal Token Generator. Supply these to the credentials field (client_id, client_secret, refresh_token, or access_token).

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector fields
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date (YYYY-MM-DD); data before this date is not replicated. Optionally set account_ids to a space-separated list of specific ad accounts to sync (omit to sync all accessible accounts), define ad_analytics_reports for custom analytics reports, set lookback_window to the number of days to re-sync analytics for late-arriving data, and num_workers to control concurrency for analytics streams.
